summary: i'm a full stack develoepr with hands on experience in naxt.js, react, python, java

experience._comment_end_date: Use 'present' if this is your current role
bullets: Achievement-focused bullet with metrics if possible (e.g. 'Reduced API latency by 40% by migrating to async handlers')

_comment_experience: Add one object per role. Most recent first. The bot sends this to Gemini for scoring.
_comment_skills: List every technical skill you want matched against job descriptions. This is the single biggest driver of the AI score. Include languages, frameworks, tools, cloud platforms, etc.
_comment_hackathons: Optional — delete the array entirely if not applicable.

job_preferences._comment_titles: Each title is used as a search query on every portal. The combo count ([Naukri N/24]) = len(titles) × len(locations). Keep this list focused.
job_preferences._comment_locations: Each location is paired with each title above for search. Use 'Remote' as a literal string for remote-only searches.
job_preferences._comment_remote_ok: If true, remote jobs are not filtered out regardless of location.
job_preferences._comment_min_salary: Set a number in the local currency unit (e.g. 600000 for 6 LPA) or leave null for no salary filter.
job_preferences._comment_max_exp: Jobs explicitly requiring MORE than this many years of experience are rejected before AI scoring. Set to a high number (e.g. 99) to disable.
job_preferences._comment_blacklist_companies: Company names (case-insensitive substring match). Jobs from these companies are rejected before AI scoring.
job_preferences._comment_blacklist_keywords: Any keyword matched in the job title OR description immediately rejects the job before AI scoring. Case-insensitive.
job_preferences._comment_min_score: Jobs scoring below this integer (0–100) go to the Failed file. Adjust based on how selective you want the bot to be.
